replication_study.hypothesis: H: There is an interaction between Level (Category/NoBrand vs Brand) and Product Type (Utilitarian vs Symbolic) on the Benefit - Symbolism difference score such that branding attenuates the category-level difference (i.e., a significant Level × ProductType interaction on mean ScoreDiff where utilitarian items show larger positive ScoreDiff in the Category condition but smaller (or reduced) ScoreDiff in the Brand condition, and symbolic items show the converse).
replication_study.study_type: Experimental (laboratory-style within/between mixed design; replication dataset collected from an online experiment following the same manipulations).

data_plan.source_type: Experimental survey / collected participant responses (online lab questionnaire)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Full available replication dataset provided (data/original/23/input/replication_data). No subset required; analysis follows original Study 1B replication design.
data_plan.sample_size: N = 637 rows in the CSV file. After attention/screening filters applied in the script, the effective sample size will be reduced; final sample size output is saved by the script (dat_collapsed.csv).
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Individual participants (ID) with repeated measures across product items (collapsed to mean ScoreDiff by ProductType per participant for the mixed ANOVA).
data_plan.access_details: Data files provided in the replication bundle (data/original/23/input/replication_data). No external restrictions indicated.

qualification.explanation: The provided dataset contains the same experimental structure and fields needed to operationalize the focal test: per-item Benefit ratings and Symbolism ratings (7-point numeric responses), condition assignment (Cond), and an items stimulus list mapping ItemCatID -> ProductType and Cond. The original paper's pre-registration/metadata describes Study 1B as an experiment manipulating Level (category vs brand) and measuring benefit vs symbolism ratings and computing difference scores (benefit - symbolism). The replication CSV contains repeated sets of utilitarian and symbolic rating columns and a stimulus ItemsList_Final.csv mapping ItemCatID and Cond to ProductType (see ItemsList_Final.csv in replication_data) and the original study description in post_registration.json which documents the Study 1 design. (References: replication data files: LeBeouf_replication_data.csv and ItemsList_Final.csv in data/original/23/input/replication_data; description of original study and outcome variables: post_registration.json under original_study.method and original_study.data.)
qualification.similarity_to_original: High fidelity: The data includes 8 products (ItemCatID 1-8) evaluated per participant with separate Benefit and Symbolism ratings that allow computing the ScoreDiff = Benefits - Symbols measure used in the original analyses (post_registration.json: 'Compute difference scores (benefit minus symbolism)'). The replication_data includes Cond (1-4) that matches how the stimuli file ItemsList_Final.csv organizes Category/NoBrand (Cond 1-2) and Brand groups (Cond 3-4). The R analysis provided in the replication_data folder (LeBeouf_JournMarketRes_2010_..._Final.R) shows the original cleaning and structure that this Python script follows.
qualification.deviation_from_original: Deviations / differences that make this a replication rather than the exact original data: The original paper samples were laboratory undergraduate participants across several studies (post_registration.json mentions Study 1B: N=206). The provided dataset appears to be a later online replication collection (N in this file = 637 rows) or an assembled dataset with more respondents. The ItemsList file is provided as CSV rather than the R script's expected Excel file; the Python script reads the CSV. The R code in the bundle used multiple column-blocks for different condition blocks; the Python script programmatically maps columns to condition blocks (mapping Cond 1/2 to block 0, Cond 3->block1, Cond 4->block2) which mirrors the R logic but uses pattern matching on column names (this is equivalent to, but not identical in implementation to, the original R col-selection approach). References: post_registration.json (study description), LeBeouf_replication_data.csv and ItemsList_Final.csv in data/original/23/input/replication_data. The R script in the bundle also used attention filters; the Python script implements the same attention and screen filters (LeBeouf_JournMarketRes...Final.R).
qualification.notes: The replication dataset is an independent data collection (different respondents and a larger sample) but uses the same experimental manipulations and measures, making it an appropriate test of robustness of the focal claim. Specific differences include file formats (CSV vs XLSX) and the presence of multiple blocks of item columns in the CSV (handled in the script).

data_plan.notes: Important caveats: column names in LeBeouf_replication_data.csv use truncated labels with repeated blocks such as '1_Util...11' etc. The Python script identifies util and symbol columns by substring matching ('Util' and 'Symbol') and assumes blocks of 8 items per block to map to Cond values. The script expects the data to be present under /app/data at runtime (see RUN POLICY). Outputs (dat_collapsed.csv, model_summary.txt, means_by_condition_producttype.csv) are saved to /app/data. If additional or different attention filters are needed it can be adjusted; the script implements the same attention/screen filters as the R script in the repository: Status==0, Attention1==7, Attention2==1, screenQ==2.

steps: Place the data files in /app/data: LeBeouf_replication_data.csv and ItemsList_Final.csv (CSV).
Run the Python replication script data/original/23/input/replication_data/LeBeouf_replication__py.py (the script expects files in /app/data).
Script performs participant filtering: Status == 0, screenQ not NA, Attention1 == 7, Attention2 == 1, screenQ == 2 to replicate original R cleaning steps.

Script removes metadata/familiarity/bipolar columns, constructs participant ID, identifies utilitarian and symbolic columns, maps blocks to conditions, and reshapes from wide to long so each participant-item has Benefits and Symbols values.
Merge long data with ItemsList_Final.csv by ItemCatID and Cond to obtain ProductType for each item (Utilitarian vs Symbolic).
Compute ScoreDiff = Benefits - Symbols for each participant-item.
Collapse to compute each participant's mean ScoreDiff by ProductType and Condition (as in the R script dat_collapsed).

Run a mixed effects model testing ScoreDiff ~ ProductType * Condition with a random intercept for participant (ID). Extract the Condition × ProductType interaction coefficient and its statistical test.
Save collapsed data and model summary outputs to /app/data for inspection and comparing to original reported statistics.
planned_method.models: A mixed-effects linear model (linear mixed model) of ScoreDiff with fixed effects ProductType, Condition, and their interaction, random intercepts for participants (ID). This acts as the operational equivalent to the R repeated-measures ANOVA (aov with Error(ID/ProductType)).
planned_method.outcome_variable: ScoreDiff (Benefits - Symbols), computed per item and collapsed to per-participant mean by ProductType (utilitarian vs symbolic) for the mixed ANOVA).
planned_method.independent_variables: Primary: Condition (Category/NoBrand vs Brand) — between-subject; ProductType (Utilitarian vs Symbolic) — within-subject; primary focus on Condition × ProductType interaction.
planned_method.control_variables: The original analysis did not include additional covariates beyond counterbalancing. The replication follows the original: no additional covariates. Attention and screening variables are used for sample cleaning (Attention1, Attention2, screenQ), matching the provided R script.
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3 (script uses pandas, numpy, statsmodels). The replication code is implemented in LeBeouf_replication__py.py and expects data under /app/data. Recommended packages: pandas, numpy, statsmodels. Execution environment (Docker image) specified below.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Estimate fixed effect coefficients for ProductType, Condition, and their interaction; primary estimate of interest is the Condition × ProductType interaction coefficient (difference of differences) and group means by Condition × ProductType.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Wald t-tests (z/t from the mixed model summary) for the fixed effect interaction; equivalently F-test in ANOVA framework. Report coefficient estimate, standard error, t-value, and p-value (two-tailed). Additionally, report descriptive means and effect-size measures (means and optionally partial eta-squared via alternative calculations).

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Rows with missing Benefit or Symbol responses will generate NaN ScoreDiff; when collapsing by participant and product type the mean will be computed across available non-NA item ScoreDiffs. The model data drop rows with NA ScoreDiff (listwise deletion for model estimation) — this mirrors the R reshape that used select_if(~ !any(is.na(.))).
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Primary test is the single pre-registered interaction (Condition × ProductType). No multiple-testing correction planned for the focal hypothesis. If additional post-hoc contrasts are tested the script uses pairwise comparisons or adjustments as needed (the R script used emmeans; the Python plan will report pairwise means and could use Tukey-style corrections if additional tests are run).
planned_method.inference_criteria: Two-tailed tests, alpha = 0.05 for statistical significance. Directionality is hypothesized (attenuation of matching advantage at brand level), but the pre-registered analysis tests the interaction for significance (consistent with original). Report p-values and coefficient signs; interpret interaction consistent with original direction (attenuation).

files.LeBeouf_replication__py.py: Python translation/replication script of the original R analysis (LeBeouf_JournMarketRes_2010_..._Final.R). It: (1) reads LeBeouf_replication_data.csv and ItemsList_Final.csv from /app/data; (2) applies the same attention and screening filters used in the R script; (3) drops metadata and unwanted columns; (4) identifies utilitarian and symbolic columns by substring matching and maps them to condition blocks; (5) creates a long-form dataset, merges with items to get ProductType, computes ScoreDiff = Benefits - Symbols; (6) collapses to mean ScoreDiff by participant × ProductType × Condition; (7) fits a mixed linear model ScoreDiff ~ ProductType * Condition with random intercept for ID; (8) writes outputs to /app/data: dat_collapsed.csv, model_summary.txt, means_by_condition_producttype.csv.
files.LeBeouf_replication_data.csv: Provided raw participant response CSV (in repository at data/original/23/input/replication_data). At runtime the script expects this file to be copied or mounted to /app/data/LeBeouf_replication_data.csv.
files.ItemsList_Final.csv: Stimulus mapping file with ItemCatID, Category name, ProductType (Utilitarian/Symbolic), Brand, BrandType, and Cond. Used to attach ProductType labels to each ItemCatID × Cond combination. The script expects this file at /app/data/ItemsList_Final.csv.

codebase.notes: I created LeBeouf_replication__py.py (Python rewrite) and placed it under data/original/23/input/replication_data. Per RUN POLICY the script reads inputs from /app/data at runtime and writes outputs to /app/data. The code was designed to match the logic and filters in the original R file. If a direct run in the environment is intended, ensure LeBeouf_replication_data.csv and ItemsList_Final.csv are available at /app/data. The script uses Python-only dependencies (pandas, numpy, statsmodels).

analysis.instructions: 1) Ensure the files LeBeouf_replication_data.csv and ItemsList_Final.csv are placed in the container at /app/data. 2) Run Python 3.10 environment with required packages installed. 3) Execute the script: python /app/data/LeBeouf_replication__py.py (script will read inputs from /app/data and write outputs to /app/data). 4) Inspect outputs produced: dat_collapsed.csv (collapsed per-participant means), model_summary.txt (mixed model output including Condition × ProductType interaction coefficient, SE, t, p), and means_by_condition_producttype.csv (means table). 5) If desired, follow-up pairwise comparisons can be run via statsmodels or exported into R for emmeans-style contrasts; the focal inference is the interaction reported in model_summary.txt.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Primary comparisons: (a) Condition × ProductType interaction coefficient (estimate, SE, t-value and p-value) vs original reported F(1,204) = 7.70, p = 0.006 (original ANOVA); (b) descriptive means of ScoreDiff by Condition × ProductType to check directionality and magnitude (e.g., mean ScoreDiff for Utilitarian items in Category vs Brand); (c) effect size approximation (Cohen's d for pairwise contrasts or partial-eta-squared equivalent via conversion from F if an ANOVA is run). The replication will compare the sign and statistical significance of the interaction and the descriptive means to evaluate whether branding attenuates the matching advantage.
